prompt: Painterly Elysian-Cathedral illustration, landscape 16:9, a dim forest sanctum interior. The frame is a vaulted gothic cathedral nave whose stone ribs are actually ancient massive tree trunks — twisted bark and limbs fusing into carved pillars that arch high overhead. Between the trunks: tall narrow stained-glass panels in leaf-motif emerald green, moss-green, and amber-gold, with thick black leading. Dappled green-gold light shafts angle down from the upper left, pooling softly on a weathered mossy flagstone floor at lower-center. Faint in the deep background, centered in the arch, a massive silhouetted Great Thornkin Tree form — just a shape, not detailed. The lower-center floor area is a CLEAR OPEN STAGE — no creatures, no figures, no text, no UI, no cards, no banners — it reads as a battlefield that the UI will sit on top of. The peripheral columns, ceiling ribs, and outer side pillars are noticeably darker than the center stage. Overall image is dimmed (dusk inside a cathedral) — target average brightness about 32%. Palette: deep forest green (#1a3826), mossy stone (#0e2216), emerald glass (#2d6a3f), amber-gold highlights (#d6a24a). Soft anti-aliasing. No hard UI edges. No text, no letters, no watermark, no logos.
compositionNotes: Lower-center is the battlefield stage — kept brighter so UI reads on top
Outer columns and ceiling darker than stage for frame-to-eye focus
No figures, text, or UI elements baked into the raster
Faint Thornkin Tree silhouette at the far rear anchors zone identity
notes: 2K landscape (2048x1152), target overall luminance 30-40% per PRD §D1.
Post-gamma adjusted (gamma 0.66) to lift average brightness from source into the 30-40% band.
If this raster is regenerated, re-run Playwright visual-regression with --update-snapshots.
